steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: The correct answer is $68$. It's given that the function $f$ is defined by $f\left(x\right)=8{x}^{3}+4$. Substituting $2$ for $x$ in this equation yields $f\left(2\right)=8{\left(2\right)}^{3}+4$, or $f\left(2\right)=8\left(8\right)+4$, which is equivalent to $f\left(2\right)=68$. Therefore, the value of $f\left(2\right)$ is $68$.
